headline: Papa John’s Stock Jumps on Reported Takeover Bid
summary: Shares of  Papa John’s International  jumped sharply Wednesday after a report that the pizza chain has attracted fresh takeover interest.  A Qatari-backed investment firm, Irth Capital Management, has submitted an offer to take Papa John’s private at about $47 a share, valuing the company at roughly $1.5 billion, according to a report in The Wall Street Journal.  The reported bid comes as Papa John’s faces sluggish sales and rising costs cut into margins.

headline: PIZZA HUT LAUNCHES HUT CRUST PLATFORM FOR CRUST LOVERS & SETS OUT TO HIRE FIRST-OF-ITS-KIND HUT CRUST CONNOISSEUR
summary: When it comes to pizza, crust isn't just a detail… it's the main event. And at Pizza Hut, crust reigns supreme. Today, Pizza Hut is officially introducing Hut Crust – a new platform celebrating the bold, recognizable crusts that have defined the brand for generations. At the center of the platform is a crave-worthy $10 large three-topping pizza2 available on fans' choice of three iconic crusts: the new and improved Hand-Tossed, Tavern Style, or Thin 'N Crispy.

summary: Looks like they still don’t know how PZZA tastes in Qatar

headline: Papa John’s Surges on Report Irth Capital Preparing New Bid
summary: The pizza chain is reviewing the bid of $47 per share, which has backing from Brookfield Asset management, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the deal.  Papa John’s stock rose as much as 23% to nearly $40 in New York trading.

headline: Did Yum!'s Q4 Beat and 4,500 New Openings Just Shift Yum! Brands' (YUM) Investment Narrative?
summary: Yum! Brands recently reported strong Q4 2025 results, with revenue and same-store sales exceeding analyst expectations, Q4 operating profit rising 12%, and more than 4,500 new restaurants opened worldwide for the year across KFC, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut. The quarter underscored Taco Bell’s 7% same-store sales growth and rapid global expansion, highlighting how Yum!’s franchise-focused model is scaling its brands while maintaining operational momentum. Next, we’ll examine how this...

headline: Devyani International to merge three subsidiaries with parent company
summary: The three subsidiaries operate more than 100 outlets, spanning dine-in locations and cloud kitchens in over 40 cities in India.
